paper_title: Identification and expression analysis of COBRA genes in pear

methods.method_name: COBRA Gene Identification and Phylogenetic Analysis
methods.description: This method involves isolating COBRA genes from the pear genome and performing phylogenetic analysis to classify them into different groups based on their exon structure.

steps.description: A total of 17 COBRA genes were isolated from pear genome.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate COBRA genes from pear genome

steps.description: Phylogenetic analysis showed that these COBRA genes were grouped into three classes.
steps.searchable_summary: perform phylogenetic analysis on COBRA genes

steps.description: Classes II and III were composed by two (COB.A and COB.B) and four (from COB.C to COB.F) groups, respectively.
The COBRA genes in class I contained four exons, the genes in class II contained two or three exons.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze exon structure of COBRA genes

methods.description: This method involves analyzing the expression patterns of COBRA genes in various pear tissues using transcriptome analysis and semi-quantitative PCR.

steps.description: Transcriptome analysis showed that the genes in class I and groups COB.C and COB.E were rarely expressed in all tested tissues.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze transcriptome data for COBRA gene expression

steps.description: A pair of primers were designed to amplify both genes specifically.
steps.searchable_summary: design primers for COBRA gene amplification

steps.description: Semi-quantitative PCR analysis showed that PbCOB.A.1 and PbCOB.A.2 were expressed in pollen, but were undetectable in root, stem, leaf, style, and fruit.
steps.searchable_summary: perform semi-quantitative PCR for COBRA gene expression
